verifier: fresh cross-agent verifier R393 (claude-opus-4-7, no prior context)
rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL
candidate_summary: BADKHN-EVAL: dual-axis evaluation diagnostic combining (a) creativity = semantic novelty vs base corpus, (b) factual accuracy vs ground-truth person-facts F, into a single score via geometric mean sqrt(c*f) so that either-axis collapse drives the score to zero.

results.title: Survey LLM Hallucination via Creativity Perspective
results.rationale: Survey explicitly frames hallucination-vs-creativity as a joint trade-off and motivates dual-axis evaluation; same dual-dimension problem statement that BADKHN-EVAL operationalises. Crosses both 0.7 thresholds.

results.title: HeuriGym Quality-Yield Index unified metric (ICLR 2026)
results.rationale: DIRECT TWIN — QYI is an explicit unified 0-1 metric that JOINTLY captures feasibility (a correctness axis) AND quality (a creativity/optimality axis) in one product-like score that goes to zero if either axis collapses. This is mechanistically the same construct as the geometric-mean dual-axis BADKHN score. Far above both thresholds.

results.title: Evaluating LLM Metrics Through Real-World Capabilities (2025)
results.rationale: Argues that no single-axis number captures multidimensional LLM reality and advocates combined multi-dimensional metrics — establishes the prior-art motivation that the candidate frames as novel. Crosses both 0.7 thresholds.

results.title: LLM Evaluation Metrics Ultimate Guide (Confident AI)
results.rationale: DIRECT TWIN — describes operational LLM metrics that combine factual-alignment and inclusion via a MIN-of-two intermediary score, which is the same conjunctive logic as the geometric mean (both penalise either-axis collapse to zero). The 'novel' joint-collapse property of BADKHN-EVAL is already an industry pattern. Crosses both 0.7.

results.title: Survey Hallucinations LLM Prompting/Model Attribution (Frontiers 2025)
results.rationale: Formalises a Generalization-Hallucination Trade-off — same dual-axis (creativity-like generalisation vs factual accuracy) phenomenon BADKHN-EVAL is designed around. Crosses both thresholds.

results.title: LLM Hallucinations Comprehensive Survey (2025/10)
results.rationale: Comprehensive survey explicitly covering the creativity-accuracy trade-off and joint evaluation considerations; subsumes the joint-axis framing of BADKHN-EVAL. Crosses both 0.7.

results.title: HalluLens LLM Hallucination Benchmark (2025/04)
results.rationale: Single-axis hallucination benchmark without an explicit creativity axis or joint-collapse penalty. Below both 0.7 thresholds.

results.title: Mathematically Evaluating Hallucinations in LLMs
results.rationale: Single-axis mathematical hallucination evaluation, no joint creativity-accuracy combination. Below thresholds.

verdict_sentence: FAIL — six of eight retrieved sources (notably HeuriGym's Quality-Yield Index and Confident AI's min-of-factual-and-inclusion metric, both direct twins) cross sem>=0.7 and func>=0.7, establishing that joint dual-axis creativity-accuracy metrics with either-axis-collapse penalty are already prior art, so BADKHN-EVAL is non-novel.
